task: backdoor attack on graph neural networks
input: graph-structured data (e.g., social networks, biological graphs)
output: poisoned training samples with trigger-embedded graphs that cause misclassification
backbone: any GNN (e.g., GCN, GAT, GraphSAGE) as victim model
loss: not specified (attack method, not a training loss)
key_idea: use motifs (frequent subgraphs) as triggers, selecting less frequent motifs for better attack effectiveness
